comments: Approved. The execution contract is tightly bounded to documentation deliverables only. Its scope explicitly limits work to generating tasks/v0.7-m3-a-feedback-taxonomy.md and defines the content of that document around four required goals: failure taxonomy, feedback ingestion, retrospective artifact modeling, and the retry-versus-improvement boundary. The allowed_files list contains only the design document, and the stop_conditions forbid modifications outside that boundary or any attempt to implement executable scripts. The out_of_scope section correctly forbids automation implementation, automatic retries, governance/review rewrites, .agent-state migration, and modifications to active workflow/dispatch.json semantics. The recon packet independently confirms the same intent: it states the objective as designing foundational data models, marks the design document as the only in-scope artifact, and excludes automation scripting, dispatch semantics changes, and MCP/server logic changes. I found no clause that expands implementation into automation, workflow behavior changes, state migration, or governance rewrites. The contract is therefore consistent with the recon packet and sufficiently constrained for the V0.7-M3-A phase.
